units.description: BST precondition: the input tree is a valid BST. (LCA on BST keys (variant 6): emphasize uniqueness of the lowest common ancestor node.)
common_failure_modes: omitting the BST precondition and solving general-tree LCA
assuming strict ordering within equal keys

units.description: Presence precondition: both query keys are present in the tree. (LCA on BST keys (variant 6): emphasize uniqueness of the lowest common ancestor node.)
common_failure_modes: returning None silently when a key is missing

units.description: Ancestor property: both query keys lie in the subtree rooted at the returned key. (LCA on BST keys (variant 6): emphasize uniqueness of the lowest common ancestor node.)
common_failure_modes: returning any common ancestor instead of the lowest one

units.description: Lowest: no proper descendant of the returned node contains both keys. (LCA on BST keys (variant 6): emphasize uniqueness of the lowest common ancestor node.)
common_failure_modes: using LCA of general trees (parent-pointer style) semantics

units.description: Key equality case: when p == q, the LCA is that same key. (LCA on BST keys (variant 6): emphasize uniqueness of the lowest common ancestor node.)
